title: Core AI Fabric Recipe
description: A conversion recipe: everything needed to turn one upstream Hugging Face model into a provenance-verified .aimodel artifact. Recipes are the unit of contribution in coreai-fabric.

id.description: Recipe id. Doubles as the coreai-catalog model id at register time, so it must not collide with an existing catalog id.

revision.description: Upstream HF commit sha the recipe was authored against. Optional: leave absent if not resolved; convert re-resolves and pins the exact revision in the conversion manifest.

license.description: Upstream license identifier as declared by the upstream repo (e.g. the HF license tag).

license_terms.description: License triage — mirrors the coreai-catalog upstream.schema vocabulary so the two repos share one contract. permissive = on the fabric allowlist (commercial_use maps to 'likely'); weak_copyleft/restricted/review_required all map to commercial_use 'check_license' (conservative); unknown = not yet triaged. Triage labels are not legal advice.

pipeline_tag.description: Upstream HF pipeline_tag at scaffold time (informational).

size_bytes.description: Upstream repo storage size in bytes as reported by the HF API at scaffold time (informational).

tool.description: Converter executable resolved on PATH at convert time (verified tools: coreai-fabric-llm-export from this repo's [convert] extra, coreai.llm.export from an apple/coreai-models checkout), OR a per-model script path inside an apple/coreai-models checkout (models/<family>/export.py) — script tools are documented but not driven by fabric; convert prints the verified manual invocation.

min_tool_version.description: Minimum version of the underlying converter library (coreai-torch) known to work for this recipe — every verified tool is a thin driver over coreai-torch. Leave absent if unknown; 0.4.1 was validated on real hardware 2026-07-03.

apple_registry_name.description: For tool `coreai.llm.export` (Apple's production exporter): the Apple model-registry short-name (e.g. `qwen3-0.6b`, from `coreai.model.registry --list-models`). When set, convert drives `coreai.llm.export <short-name>` and lets Apple's TESTED compression preset resolve — this produces the production KV-cache chat asset that passes parity. `precision`/`quantization` are then documentation only (the preset wins). Absent = raw HF-ID export (requires `--experimental`, uses this recipe's precision/quantization, and typically does NOT clear the parity bar).

compression_config.description: HIGH-FIDELITY path: a repo-relative path to a coreai-opt `quantization_config` YAML (e.g. `quant/int8_absmax_perblock32.yaml`). When set, convert drives `coreai.llm.export <hf_repo> --experimental --compute-precision <precision> --compression-config <path>` — used for the int8 tier that is ~lossless on models where Apple's int4 registry preset is not (root-cause study 2026-07-03). Mutually exclusive with `apple_registry_name`.

args.description: Extra converter arguments passed through verbatim as --key value pairs (e.g. platform: macOS). For script tools these document the script's verified flags.

quantization.description: Passed to the converter as --compression (verified flag name; e.g. none, 4bit, or a coreai-opt YAML preset stem).

precision.description: Passed to the converter as --compute-precision. Verified vocabulary: float16, bfloat16, float32.

action.description: VLA/robot-policy conversion (bundle_kind: action). Declares the split-export shape + the policy's I/O so the host can drive the sampler loop and action_parity can verify it. A policy is inert without its embodiment — these fields are load-bearing, never guessed.

embodiment.description: Target robot family (e.g. so100_so101_6dof). Honest label of what the action chunk actuates — NOT a claim of task success.

robot_required.description: Always true for a policy: the output is inert without a matching robot.

convention.description: Coordinate convention (e.g. joint_position_delta); per-embodiment, from the upstream card, never guessed.

num_steps.description: Fixed sampler steps (e.g. 10). Part of the asset's identity + parity signature — a reduced-step variant is a DIFFERENT model with its own measured action_parity.

normalization.description: Un-normalization scheme per stream + where the stats ship (a bundle sidecar). A silent stats mismatch produces meaningless actions — action_parity hash-checks it.

graphs.description: Split-export graphs (e.g. encode, denoise_step) — each separately exported so the host drives the N-step loop and num_steps is a runtime input, not a baked constant.

variant.description: Inference engine variant. coreai-sequential drives dynamic-shape bundles (standard); coreai-pipelined uses static S=1 decode for 3.5x speedup (requires static-shape export); auto = factory detects from model structure (default).

engine.description: Tier 3: engine variant selection for the exported bundle. When variant=coreai-pipelined, the export script must produce static-shape decode graphs (S=1).

custom_kernels.description: Tier 3: custom Metal kernels to bundle with the export (3x int8 speedup pattern from the zoo). Each entry declares a kernel source + entry point.

replacement_for.description: Which stock op this kernel replaces (e.g. 'matmul_int8').

chunk_size_tokens.description: Token chunk size for KV cache chunking. Works around MPSGraph overflow on large models (zoo pattern).

host_cache_chunking.description: Tier 3: for models that overflow MPSGraph during on-device specialization, chunk the KV cache host-side so it fits. Zoo workaround for >30GB models.

format_version.description: .aimodel format version the bundle is expected to declare. Leave absent if unknown.

bundle_files.description: Paths (relative to the .aimodel bundle root) that must exist after conversion. Checked by verify Gate A.

checks.description: Structural checks run offline by `coreai-fabric verify` (implementable without the Apple runtime).

metric.description: Parity metric vs the upstream model. `greedy_parity` = the RUNNABLE Gate B for a production stateful `coreai.llm.export` asset: drive its real KV-cache decode and, teacher-forced along the fp32 reference's greedy path, measure per-token argmax agreement (the community's 'X/Y token-exact vs fp32'). Runs on macOS 26 via the coreai-core Python runtime (slow → opt-in). `benchmark_accuracy` = task accuracy (e.g. tinyMMLU) via Apple's `coreai.llm.eval`, which is a stub in coreai-models 0.1.0 (not_run until it lands). `per_token_logit_cosine`/`graph_output_cosine` = raw fidelity of a STATIC-graph export. `action_parity` = the RUNNABLE, robot-free Gate B for a VLA/robot policy: fix the flow-matching sampler's initial noise (deterministic, like greedy_parity teacher-forces), feed recorded (images,state,instruction) from a LeRobot dataset, and compare the predicted action chunk vs the torch reference (min chunk cosine + per-dim MAE). Proves the export is NUMERICALLY FAITHFUL to the source policy — NOT real-world task success. See docs/parity-protocol.md.

tolerance.description: Allowed slack below threshold before hard failure.

max_action_mae.description: action_parity only (legacy, scale-naive): require the worst per-dimension ABSOLUTE normalized action MAE to stay <= this. Superseded by max_relative_action_mae for checkpoints whose normalized-action range differs from ~unit scale; kept for backward compatibility.

max_relative_action_mae.description: action_parity only (preferred): require the worst per-dimension MAE divided by the GLOBAL action RMS (normalized MAE) to stay <= this. Scale-invariant and robust to both large-action and near-zero-action checkpoints (per-dim normalization explodes on unused dims). When present, verify gates on this instead of the absolute max_action_mae. The chunk-cosine threshold remains the primary gate.

greedy_token_exact.description: LLMs only: additionally require greedy-decoded tokens to match the upstream exactly.

protocol.description: The measurement SIGNATURE for the Gate-B number (RFC Phase 0, finding F2). 'The Gate-B number' is several incommensurable quantities (match-rate vs cosine, per-row vs flattened, synthetic vs recorded, fp16 vs fp32); without this block a raw value cannot be compared to anything. Written by `coreai-fabric verify` from the harness report — not hand-authored. All fields optional, but when present they are validated.

n_obs.description: Number of observations (prompts/frames) the number was measured over.

seed.description: Seed fixing the deterministic input set / sampler noise.

input_protocol.description: Where the inputs came from. synthetic = torch.rand/seeded random (the smolvla 0.02-cosine catastrophe is the standing warning); recorded = real dataset frames/pixels; mixed = a documented blend. Replaces the schema description that falsely claimed recorded LeRobot inputs everywhere.

reference_dtype.description: Precision of the torch reference the asset was compared against.

granularity.description: How the per-observation metric was aggregated. flattened = whole-tensor cosine (structurally inflated for wide outputs); per_row = per-row min; per_token = per-token min. Leaderboards exist only within a cell that matches this.

graph_boundary.description: What the number spans (e.g. 'encode→denoise', 'whole-LM', 'action head'). A reduced-boundary export (head-only) cannot cite a whole-graph number.

loaded_on_ane.description: Deployability signal, DISTINCT from parity (finding F11): an AR generator reloading N multi-GB programs per token can pass cosine while being undeployable. True only when the composed bundle loaded + ran on the ANE at the claimed envelope.

waivers.description: Named relaxations applied (e.g. 'near_zero_action'). Surfaced into the catalog evaluation, never silently marks passed-below-bar.

hf_target_namespace.description: The publisher's OWN Hugging Face namespace (user or org). Fabric never hosts weights.

repo_name.description: Target HF repo name for the published artifact.

collection.description: Optional HF Collection title under the namespace to add the published model to (idempotent, exists_ok). Groups a publisher's CoreAI work — HF namespaces are flat, so Collections are the native way to separate/organize. e.g. `CoreAI · Apple on-device`.

variant.description: Optional quantization-tier name (e.g. `int4`, `int8`). When set, this recipe is one VARIANT of a multi-tier repo: its bundle uploads to `<variant>/` inside the shared `repo_name`, and the model card compares all sibling variants (same namespace+repo_name) — the community layout (one repo, int4/+int8/, one card).

catalog.description: Fields needed to generate the coreai-catalog entries at register time. Optional at draft stage; required before `coreai-fabric register`. The catalog rejects `unknown` for the runtime facts and requires bundle_kind + min_os on every model, so the recipe must declare them here — fabric is the converter and is the authoritative source for these conversion-time facts.

context_length.description: Max context window of the produced asset (the registry preset's resolved value, e.g. 8192 for qwen3-0.6b/macOS). Surfaced in the model card's facts table so a user can judge fit.

languages.description: Languages the model supports (BCP-47/HF codes, e.g. [en, zh] or [multilingual]). Emitted as the HF card `language:` frontmatter to drive HF's language facet. Only declare what the upstream card states.

bundle_kind.description: Mirrors the catalog model.schema bundle_kind enum. The catalog test suite requires bundle_kind on every model. Choose the kind that matches the model's task (e.g. an LLM chat model = llm, a document-OCR model = ocr, a per-token labeler = token-classification).

min_os.description: Minimum OS the .aimodel runtime requires. For apple-core-ai this is macOS/iOS 27.0, grounded in apple/coreai-models Package.swift platforms: [.macOS("27.0"), .iOS("27.0")]. Defaults to 27.0/27.0 at register time if absent.

runtime_facts.description: Runtime characteristics of the converted bundle. The catalog's audit REJECTS `unknown` for these four, so they must be real booleans. A standard clean coreai-fabric export runs on the stock CoreAIRunner with no custom Metal kernel, no zoo-style source patch, and no separate AOT step (the export IS the compile) — declare true/false honestly for THIS recipe's conversion.

stock_runtime.description: Runs on the stock CoreAIRunner without runtime modification.

patch_required.description: Requires a source patch to the runtime (zoo-style). A clean fabric export is false.

aot_required.description: Requires a separate ahead-of-time compile beyond the export.

capabilities.description: What the model DOES — its tasks/behaviors. Controlled vocabulary mirrored from the catalog model schema; extend it there first, never ad-hoc. Architecture/inference properties (moe, mla, hybrid-llm, diffusion-lm, speculative-decoding) are NOT capabilities — put those in `traits`.

traits.description: What the model IS inside — architecture or inference technique, orthogonal to what it does. Optional; most models have none. Controlled vocabulary mirrored from the catalog model schema.

architecture.description: Mirrors the catalog model.schema architecture enum.

runner.description: Mirrors the catalog model.schema runner enum. Use unknown until verified on-device.

status.description: Pipeline stage. Advanced only by the corresponding fabric command. `failed` = a conversion/parity attempt ran and did not pass (RFC Phase 1, F8); `blocked` = blocked on an external ceiling (ANE 0x10004 program ceiling, license, toolchain skew). Both carry a committed attempts/<id>.jsonl record of why.

published.description: Written back by `coreai-fabric publish` after a successful upload. Required when status is published or registered.

revision.description: HF commit sha of the published artifact repo.

catalog_pr.description: URL of the open catalog PR written back by `register` after `gh pr create`. Status stays `published` until a human MERGES this PR; `register --mark-merged` then flips status to `registered`. Absent until a PR is opened.

lerobot.description: LeRobot-specific metadata for lerobot-coreai integration. When present, publish writes lerobot-coreai.json into the HF artifact alongside parity-report.json. Absent for non-LeRobot models.

version.description: LeRobot version the source policy was trained with (e.g. '0.6.0').

public_import_path.description: Canonical LeRobot public import path for the policy (if any).
